title: Multi-Zone PID Temperature Array Controller
description: Four-zone heater controller using MAX31865 PT100 RTDs, per-zone pid_heater_control loops with thermal decoupling, SSR round-robin arbitration preventing rail overload, and MODBUS RTU setpoint interface over RS-485.
objective: Hold 4 zones to individual setpoints within ±0.5°C with decoupling correction; prevent shared rail overload via arbitration; expose readings via MODBUS.
constraints: Arduino Mega; 4x MAX31865 SPI CS=6-9; 4x SSR-40DA on pins 2-5; MAX485 RS-485 on UART1; shared 24 V rail ≤20 A.
supporting_fact_titles: Multi-Zone Thermal Decoupling in Array Heater Systems

element_usage_notes.4-Channel SSR-40DA Heater Bank: One SSR active per 100 ms arbiter slot to stay within 20 A rail limit.
element_usage_notes.MAX31865 PT100 RTD Array: Reads 4 zone temperatures at 10 Hz; fault register triggers zone shutdown.
element_usage_notes.Zone Priority Round-Robin Arbiter: Selects highest-error zone for each SSR slot via zone_arbitrate.
element_usage_notes.MAX485 RS-485 MODBUS RTU Slave: Allows PLC/SCADA to read temperatures and write per-zone setpoints.

element_interactions.description: Temperature errors per zone fed to zone_arbitrate for slot selection.
Arbiter enables one SSR output per 100 ms control tick.
MODBUS holding registers update per-zone PID setpoints used by arbiter.
RTD readings populate MODBUS input registers for SCADA readback.
